sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.45 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite$p$-group of maximal class such that all its irreducible characters are induced from linear characters of normal subgroups. Let$S$be the set of the derived subgroups of the members of the central series of$G$. Is there a logarithmic bound for the derived length of$G$in terms of$|S|$?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
